name: Jack Dempsey
abstract: William Harrison "Jack" Dempsey (June 24, 1895 – May 31, 1983), nicknamed Kid Blackie and The Manassa Mauler, was an American boxer who competed from 1914 to 1927, and was world heavyweight champion from 1919 to 1926. He is ranked sixth on The Ring magazine's list of all-time heavyweights and fourth among its Top 100 Greatest Punchers, w…

about_quotes.speaker: John Budd
about_quotes.evidence: I went over to Philadelphia because of the fight between Jack Dempsey and Gene Tunney. I didn’t go to the fight but I listened to it on a radio in a drug store.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Budd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: go across the street, next to Jack Dempsey's hang out there and drink before we go to Harlem

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: Club Alabam had such audience always as George Olson, Paul Whiteman, Vincent Lopez, Gloria Swanson, Firpo, Jack Dempsey and all

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: And they were favorites of people like Jack Dempsey, the Rothschilds, Chrysler, important people...

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: Then they hired us over at Jack Dempsey's place, over on 8th Avenue by Madison Square Garden.

about_quotes.speaker: Jeff Riddick
